Module 1 of 7
Accessing HubSpot

HubSpot is Rebuilt's CRM — the system where every acquisition deal lives from first contact through closing. As an Acquisition Associate, your work in HubSpot starts the moment you save an offer in Property Lookup and continues until the deal moves down the funnel to other teams.

The good news: you only need to master one view. Everything you need is on the Deal Page.

Logging In

Go to app.hubspot.lo.com and sign in using SSO with your Rebuilt email credentials. You do not need a separate HubSpot password.

Step 1 — Go to the HubSpot login page
Step 2 — Enter your @rebuilt.com email and click Continue
Step 3 — Click "Sign in with SSO" — no password required

Once logged in, you'll see your default dashboard view — but you won't be spending much time there. The fastest and most reliable way to get to a deal is through Property Lookup.

Best Practice: Use the Property Lookup Link

Every deal in Property Lookup has a HubSpot link in the top bar. Click that link — it takes you directly to the correct Deal Page without any searching. This is always faster than manually searching for an address in HubSpot.

Module 1 of 7
What HubSpot Is — and Isn't — Your Job

HubSpot can feel overwhelming because there's a lot going on. Tabs, panels, dashboards, pipelines. Here's what you need to know up front: most of it is not your responsibility.

As an Acquisition Associate, your HubSpot work is scoped to three things:

  • 1
    Managing and following up on your deals through the Deal Page
  • 2
    Creating and sending PandaDocs contracts through HubSpot (not directly through PandaDocs)
  • 3
    Monitoring your Acquisition Dashboard for tasks, protected deals, and pipeline status
You Do NOT Move Deal Stages

Deal stages (Prospect, Under Contract, Marketing, etc.) are moved by Virtual Assistants after all required checks are complete — not by you. You can see where a deal is, but don't move it yourself, even after getting a signed contract.

Module 1 — Knowledge Check
Check Your Understanding
Module 2 of 7
The Deal Page — Left Panel

When you open a deal in HubSpot, the page is divided into three panels — Left, Middle, and Right. The Left Panel is your deal's identity card. It tells you where a deal is and gives you key context before you pick up the phone.

Property Address
Confirms which property this deal is for. Always verify you're on the right deal before taking action.
Deal Stage
Shows where the deal is in the pipeline (e.g., Prospect, RCC Hot Lead, Under Contract, Marketing). Remember: you read this — you don't change it.
Close Date
The projected closing date. Populated automatically — not something you typically edit.
Prospect Stage = You Can Follow Up

When a deal is in Prospect stage, you have an active role — this is a lead you can and should be working. Other stages indicate the deal has moved further along the funnel.

The Deal Page left panel: deal name, pipeline, deal stage, action buttons, and the "About This Deal" section where you confirm your name as Deal Owner.
Module 2 of 7
About This Deal & Key Fields

Below the stage, the "About This Deal" section contains fields you'll actually interact with. Here's what matters most:

Deal Owner
This must be you for every deal you work. When you pull a deal from Property Lookup, it should auto-populate with your name. If it doesn't, update it — this is how you take ownership and protect the deal from others following up.
Item ID
A unique identifier used in reporting. You don't need to do anything with it — just know it's there if someone from ops or reporting asks for it.
Appointment Details / Recap
If this deal had a previous appointment, notes about what happened are here. Critical for follow-up — always check why a deal was previously held or lost before calling the seller. Newer deals will show this info as a Note in the middle panel instead.
Close Loss Information
If a deal was in the "Back Out" stage, the reason is here. Before re-engaging a seller, always check this — especially for situations where the seller owes more than they're willing to accept (e.g., owes $60K but wants to net $40K).
Appointment Details: includes Follow Up Score, seller Motivation, ARV, Repair Estimates, MAO, Offer amount, and Appointment Recap notes from previous contacts.
Module 2 of 7
Marketing & Contract Info

The lower portion of the Left Panel tracks what happens to a deal after it leaves your hands. You won't change these fields, but knowing what they mean helps you understand where your deal stands.

Marketing Information
Shows when the deal went live on the marketplace and the most recent date it was actively marketed to buyers.
Ordered Photos Date
Photos are ordered once a legally binding contract is signed (all pages complete). This date confirms that process has started — key for virtual deals where photos are the buyer's first look at the property.
Contract Terms
Populates automatically after a contract is fully signed. Shows the agreed price and terms.
Assignment
Appears once a buyer has been found and assigned to the deal. Includes buyer terms and any flat fee for MLS listing if applicable.
Left Panel Rule of Thumb

The only section of the Left Panel you'll actively edit is "About This Deal" — specifically confirming your name as Deal Owner. Everything else is informational or auto-populated.

Marketing Information: shows when the deal first went live, the most recent marketing attempt, number of marketing attempts, and the Ordered Photos date.
Contract Terms: populates after a signed contract — shows the Original and Current Acquisition Price, Disposition Price, Assignment Fee, and Exit Strategy.
Assignment Details: appears once a buyer is assigned — includes Assignment Date, Disposition Closing Date, Type of Funding, and Buyer Exit Strategy.
Flat Fee: tracks MLS listing details if the deal goes to flat fee — including Flat Fee Status, Date Listing Live, and MLS pricing. This section is managed by the dispo team, not AAs.
Module 2 — Knowledge Check
Check Your Understanding
Module 3 of 7
Notes, Tasks & the Call Function

In the upper middle panel of the Deal Page, you'll find four action buttons: Note, Task, Email, and Call. Of these, three are essential to your daily workflow. Here's how each one works — and when to use it.

Note
A text entry visible to everyone with access to the deal (including your sales leader and Dustin). Use Notes to log what happened during a call — what the seller said, where things stand, key information. Notes are not reminders — they won't prompt you to follow up.
Click "Note" in the action bar to log what happened during a seller call. Notes are visible to your entire team including your sales leader.
The Note modal: type your interaction summary and optionally check "Create a To-do task to follow up" to link a task directly to the note.
Task ← Preferred for Follow-Up
Creates an actionable to-do with a due date assigned to you. Use Tasks whenever you need to remember to call a seller at a specific time. Tasks show up in your Acquisition Dashboard and trigger notifications — Notes do not.
Click "Task" to create a follow-up reminder with a due date. Unlike Notes, Tasks appear in your dashboard and send notifications.
The Task modal: enter a descriptive task name, set the Activity Date to your target follow-up date, and confirm it is assigned to you. Task Type should be "To-do".
Email
Infrequently used by most AAs. Your team lead (Cody) will help you connect your email to HubSpot if needed.
Call (Zoom Phone)
Clicking "Call" redirects you to Zoom Phone and auto-logs the call to the deal's activity feed — including a recording. This is the recommended method for all seller follow-up calls.
Module 3 of 7
Notes vs. Tasks — The Key Difference

This distinction trips people up, so let's make it concrete.

Scenario: You just spoke with a seller

She said she's interested but wants to talk to her daughter first. She asked you to call back in three days.

What to do:

  • 1
    Create a Note → Log what she said: "Seller interested, needs to speak with daughter. Requested callback in 3 days."
  • 2
    Create a Task → Set due date 3 business days out, assign to yourself. Title it something like "Follow up — seller spoke with daughter re: offer."
Why Both?

The Note is the record. The Task is the reminder. If you only write a Note, nothing will prompt you to follow up — and with dozens of deals in play, you will forget. Create both every time.

When creating a Task: it will automatically be assigned to your name. Set the due date to the appropriate number of business days. The task title should be descriptive enough that you know exactly what to do when you see it in your dashboard.

Module 3 — Knowledge Check
Check Your Understanding
Module 4 of 7
Middle Panel — Activities & the Seller Info Worksheet

The Middle Panel is your deal's activity log. Everything that happens — calls, notes, stage changes, automated system events — flows through here. The key is knowing what to look at and what to filter out.

Filter to Notes and Calls

The "All Activity" view includes a lot of automated back-end events that aren't relevant to you. Instead, filter the activity feed to show only Notes and Calls. This gives you a clean view of every human interaction on the deal.

Default view: the Activity filter has all 26 activity types selected — a noisy mix of automations, system events, and integrations that makes it hard to find real interactions.
Filtered view: deselect everything except Calls and Notes (shown here as 2 of 26). This surfaces only human interactions — exactly what you need when reviewing a deal.

Within the calls view, you'll see a recording link for each logged Zoom Phone call. You can't listen while you're live on a call — but after you hang up, reviewing the recording is a great way to catch details you may have missed.

The activity feed also shows you who else has been involved with the deal — if multiple associates have touched a lead, you'll see all of their logged activity here. This matters for context when you're picking up a cold deal.

Module 4 of 7
Seller & Buyer Info Worksheet

Below the activity feed in the Middle Panel is the Seller & Buyer Info Worksheet. This section auto-populates with data from the third page of the AB Contract — the seller information sheet your seller fills out (or that you fill out with them) during the contract walk-through.

Critical: Always Create Contracts Inside HubSpot

The worksheet only auto-populates when the contract is created through HubSpot — not through PandaDocs directly. If you create a PandaDoc contract by going to PandaDocs.com instead of through the HubSpot right panel, the data connection breaks and nothing populates here. Always create contracts inside HubSpot.

Why does this matter? The worksheet gives you and your team a quick, structured snapshot of seller details without having to open the full contract PDF. It's how Dustin (underwriting), your sales leader, and the dispo team stay aligned on the deal's specifics.

Once a buyer is assigned to the deal, buyer information will also appear in this section.

AB Contract Terms: auto-populates with contract dates, acquisition price, due diligence date, and amendment fields once a PandaDoc contract created through HubSpot is signed.
Property Ownership & Status: captures title holder details, probate status, co-owners, and how the property was acquired — critical context for your team and underwriting.
Primary Seller Info Worksheet: seller contact details, marital status, spouse info, occupancy type, and post-occupancy arrangements — filled out together with the seller during the contract walk-through.
Module 4 — Knowledge Check
Check Your Understanding
Module 5 of 7
Right Panel — Contacts

The Right Panel runs along the right side of the Deal Page. The first section you'll use regularly is Contacts — it pulls in the seller's information from Property Lookup and is where you manage everyone connected to the deal.

Email Addresses & PandaDocs

A valid email address in the Contacts section is required to send a PandaDoc. If the seller didn't provide one during your call, add it here before creating the contract. Once you add it in HubSpot, it auto-populates into your PandaDoc send — saving you a step.

No Email? Use a Dummy Address
If the seller doesn't have an email, enter [email protected] as a placeholder. This keeps the system happy — but remember, the seller won't receive the PandaDoc at that address. Send it via text message instead.
Adding Additional Contacts
If the seller's daughter, spouse, or another decision-maker is relevant to the deal, add them as a separate contact. Use a descriptive name (e.g., "Dianne (daughter) Mode") so anyone viewing the deal understands the relationship immediately.
Clicking Email or Phone
Click a contact's email address to open the HubSpot email composer, or click their phone number to dial via Zoom Phone. Both actions auto-log to the deal's activity feed.
Note on Syncing
Changes made to contact info in HubSpot do not sync back to Property Lookup. If you update an email address in HubSpot, it won't appear on the Property Lookup side.
Contacts panel: the primary seller (Cody Carver, tagged "Seller") and an additional contact (Dianne, the daughter) both listed. Click the teal email link or phone number to contact them directly — both auto-log to the deal.
Module 5 of 7
Right Panel — Attachments & PandaDocs

Below Contacts in the Right Panel are two more sections you'll use regularly: Attachments and PandaDocs.

Attachments
A repository for any files associated with the deal — signed contracts mailed in rather than via PandaDoc, scanned documents, or other supporting materials. If a seller mails a signed contract instead of signing digitally, it gets scanned and uploaded here.
Attachments panel: stores uploaded files tied to the deal. Contracts that were signed by mail (rather than via PandaDoc) are scanned and attached here so the full deal record stays in one place.
PandaDocs — Draft
A contract that was created but never sent to the seller.
PandaDocs — Sent
A contract that has been sent and is awaiting the seller's signature.
PandaDocs — Expired
A contract that was sent but not signed before the validity window closed (typically 48 hours, or 24 hours if tightened for urgency).
PandaDocs — Completed
A fully signed, legally binding contract. This is what triggers photo ordering and the transition toward under contract stage.
Always Create PandaDocs From Within HubSpot

Click "Create a document" from the PandaDocs section in the right panel — not from PandaDocs.com directly. Creating it here ensures all deal data points are connected and the Seller Info Worksheet populates correctly.

You can also view what the seller is doing in real time once a PandaDoc is sent — you'll see when they open it, which pages they're on, and which fields they've completed. This visibility helps during the live contract walk-through.

PandaDocs panel: shows all contracts associated with the deal with their status (Draft, Sent, Expired, Completed). Always create new contracts from here — never go to PandaDocs.com directly.
Module 5 — Knowledge Check
Check Your Understanding
Module 6 of 7
The Acquisition Dashboard

The Acquisition Dashboard is your command center. Access it from the dashboard icon (the three signal bars) in the left nav of HubSpot. Bookmark the URL so you can get to it in one click.

This is where you monitor the health of your entire pipeline — tasks due, deals at risk, contracts on hold, and deals moving through the funnel. Here's what each section means:

Protected Deals Count
The number of deals that are "protected" under your name — meaning no other associate can follow up on them because you've been consistently contacting the seller. Displayed both as a count and as a list.
Deals With No Contact in 6 Days
A warning list. If you haven't touched base with a seller in 6 days, this is your alert. On day 7, the deal drops your name and becomes accessible to others. Protect your deals by following up every 6 days or fewer.
Overdue Tasks
Tasks with a due date that has passed. These are follow-ups you set for yourself and didn't complete — address them immediately.
Upcoming Tasks
Tasks coming due soon. Use this as your daily planning tool — click any task to jump directly to that deal's page.
Module 6 of 7
Dashboard — Pipeline & Status Views

Below the task sections, the dashboard shows you the full lifecycle of your deals — from active follow-up through closed and back-out.

Contract On Hold
Deals in this bucket are waiting for a specific action before they can move to marketing. Common reasons: photo access issues, seller unreachable, or a retrade is needed. Your job is to resolve these and move them forward.
Retrade Task Pending
Created by Dustin (underwriting) when a price reduction is needed after going under contract. The task will include a projected reduction target and Dustin's notes on why. You need to go back to the seller and renegotiate.
Marketing
Deals that are active on the marketplace. Shows how many of your deals are currently being shown to buyers.
Buyer Assigned
A buyer has been secured. The deal is moving toward closing — your work here is largely done.
All Access List
Leads that no one has followed up on — they're open to any associate. The 59 dialer handles these automatically; manual follow-up is not required (or recommended).
Back Out
Deals where either the seller or Rebuilt withdrew. Always investigate the reason before re-engaging — close loss info in the Left Panel tells you why.
Module 6 — Knowledge Check
Check Your Understanding
Module 7 of 7
The End-to-End Workflow

Now let's put it all together. Here's the complete flow from live call to signed contract — every step in sequence, and what you're doing in each system.

1
You're on the phone with the seller in Property Lookup
Run the offer, build motivation, complete the discovery. Fill in all fields in Property Lookup — including the seller's email if they provide it.
2
Seller is ready to move forward → Hit "Save Offer"
This single action creates the deal in HubSpot and makes the HubSpot link clickable. The deal enters at "RCC Hot Lead" stage. Do not click HubSpot before hitting Save Offer — it won't be live yet.
3
Click the HubSpot link → go directly to the Deal Page
Confirm you are listed as Deal Owner. If no email was captured in Property Lookup, add it now in the Contacts section on the right panel.
4
Create the PandaDoc contract from within HubSpot
Scroll to the PandaDocs section in the right panel → click "Create a document." Do NOT go to PandaDocs.com directly. The data connection only works through HubSpot.
5
Walk the seller through the contract live on the phone
Keep them on the call. Guide them through the Seller Info Sheet (page 3) together — it's a lot of questions and sellers tend to stall if left to do it alone. Get page-by-page signatures live.
✓
Contract signed → deal moves forward
The VA team takes it from here — they'll verify everything and move the deal to Under Contract. Photos get ordered. Your job is to stay available and responsive if anything needs to be resolved.
Module 7 of 7
Key Rules to Remember

Before the final knowledge check, here are the most important rules from everything you've covered:

Always Do
Confirm you're Deal Owner on every deal
Use Tasks (not just Notes) for follow-up reminders
Create PandaDocs from inside HubSpot
Use the Call button for seller calls
Follow up every 6 days to protect your deals
Check Close Loss info before re-engaging
Never Do
Manually move deal stages yourself
Create contracts directly in PandaDocs.com
Send a PandaDoc without confirming the email address
Put sensitive seller info in Notes visible to everyone
Let 7+ days pass without seller contact
Get Help Via
Your Acquisition Channels (Slack)
Rebuilt Knowledge Base portal
Cody — Zoom Phone & email setup
Dustin — underwriting questions
DK or Mike — HubSpot workflow questions
Questions? Use Your Acquisition Channel

Post questions in your Acquisition Slack channel — not in direct messages to individuals. More people can see and answer your question faster, and others with the same question benefit too.
